lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing or reacting phosphatic fertilizer materials for external customers under toll, private-label, or customer-specific nutrient programs.
lens.excluded_activities: Large integrated phosphate mines and fertilizer complexes selling standard commodity output, captive plants, mixing-only firms classified in 325314, distributors and applicators, animal-feed-only producers, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring industrial or agricultural supply priced per ton, batch, or contracted capacity, with specifications for phosphate content and product form and periodic phosphate rock, sulfur, ammonia, energy, or freight repricing.
lens.deviation_from_default: The NAICS definition includes both phosphatic-material manufacture and subsequent mixing, but most output is commodity product. The lens is narrowed to repeat toll, private-label, and customer-specific manufacturing to maintain a coherent outsourced-service population.

a.bridge: BLS lists chemical equipment operators, first-line production supervisors, chemical plant operators, industrial machinery mechanics, and related physical roles among the largest occupations in chemical manufacturing groups including 3253. I map planning, laboratory and quality review, environmental records, maintenance knowledge, inventory, procurement, and customer documentation to AI exposure while leaving acid production, reaction, granulation, material movement, sampling, repairs, and safety-critical control largely unexposed. Broad chemical-manufacturing data may overstate office-like exposure in capital-intensive phosphate facilities.
a.rationale: Phosphoric acid and phosphate fertilizer production is physical, continuous, and regulated. AI opportunity concentrates in records, quality, maintenance planning, scheduling, compliance, and commercial support rather than direct process labor.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted task inventories and time studies from independent phosphatic fertilizer plants, including current DCS, laboratory, and environmental-reporting automation, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Occupation evidence covers a broader chemical-manufacturing population.
Plant design and vertical integration substantially change the task mix.

rho.rationale: AI assistance can be deployed in planning, records, laboratory review, maintenance, and customer workflows over five years, but process validation, cybersecurity, hazardous acid handling, emissions controls, quality release, and workforce change preserve human review and slow implementation.
rho.change_evidence: Audited deployments at phosphoric-acid and phosphate-fertilizer plants reporting accepted AI outputs, labor hours removed, false-alert and override rates, product quality, and compliance outcomes would materially change the range.
caveats: No public study directly measures five-year AI implementation in this industry.
Implementation is likely much higher in documentation than in safety-critical process control.

e.rationale: US phosphate fertilizer production is concentrated in integrated, commodity-oriented facilities. Only a small number of independent toll, private-label, or customer-specific manufacturers plausibly meet the recurring outsourced-service and LMM-control lens.
e.change_evidence: A complete roster of the injected LMM firms coded from beneficial ownership, plant integration, customer contracts, private-label disclosures, tolling activity, and management interviews would directly measure eligibility.
caveats: EPA reports only 13 facilities subject to at least one phosphoric-acid or phosphate-fertilizer air rule, but this is a regulatory universe rather than the full NAICS population.
No public data identify the contract-manufacturing share.

s5.bridge: Census reports that 51% of responding US employer-business owners were age 55 or older in 2018. I translate broad owner-aging pressure into a five-year qualifying-control-transfer probability and reduce it sharply for integrated corporate ownership, strategic transactions, family transfers, internal reorganizations, closures, and significant environmental diligence. The all-industry statistic likely overstates individual-owner succession exposure for concentrated phosphate assets.
s5.rationale: Smaller eligible formulators can face succession, but major phosphatic assets are concentrated and integrated, and liability or reserve issues may prevent an arm's-length transferable-company sale.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, Capital IQ, Axial, or GF Data transactions for US phosphate fertilizer and specialty nutrient producers, reconciled to beneficial ownership, owner age, facility liabilities, and transaction form, would materially change this estimate.
caveats: Owner age is not industry- or size-specific and does not measure control sales.
A sale of a plant or mine division may not constitute transfer of an eligible standalone firm.

q.rationale: Commodity phosphate benchmarks, per-ton tenders, ammonia and sulfur inputs, global capacity, and annual farm purchasing share most savings with customers. Local logistics, customer qualification, specialty grades, reliable capacity, and co-product economics allow partial retention.
q.change_evidence: A representative set of tolling and private-label contracts showing open-book clauses, input escalators, renewal cadence, cost-down requirements, minimum volumes, freight treatment, and bid outcomes would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No public contract sample was available.
Specialty liquid or customer-specific products may retain much more than commodity DAP, MAP, or phosphoric acid.

d5.bridge: USGS apparent phosphate-rock consumption is an upstream measure, not constant-quality demand for outsourced phosphatic manufacturing. It declined from an estimated 22.5 million tons in 2024 to 21.0 million in 2025, while US marketable production stayed around 20 million tons and imports rose over recent years. USDA reports phosphate averaged 19% of US fertilizer consumption volume from 2006-23. I use these data to anchor mild domestic contraction; rock consumption can diverge from finished-fertilizer demand through inventories, imports, exports, and product mix.
d5.rationale: Phosphorus remains essential for crop nutrition, but declining domestic reserves and ore grade, increased imports, improved nutrient management, and global capacity expansion pressure US production. The base implies moderate rather than catastrophic contraction.
d5.change_evidence: US apparent consumption and trade for DAP, MAP, phosphoric acid, and specialty phosphate fertilizers; USDA crop acreage and application rates; mine and plant closure schedules; and qualifying firms' contracted backlog would materially change the range.
caveats: Phosphate rock is an upstream input and includes exports and nonfertilizer uses.
Global P2O5 consumption growth does not necessarily benefit US outsourced manufacturers.

o.rationale: Any remaining phosphate-fertilizer quantity requires an accountable operator to convert phosphate rock and sulfur into acid and fertilizer, manage emissions and process hazards, test nutrient content, and release physical product. Software cannot self-serve phosphate material.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of broad customer insourcing, agronomic or biological substitutes eliminating mined phosphorus, or certified autonomous plants without an accountable manufacturer would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The operator role may consolidate into large integrated or foreign producers even if it remains physically necessary.
Labor automation is captured in a and rho and is not deducted again here.

narrative.executive_view: The qualifying opportunity is a very small customer-specific subset of a concentrated, capital-intensive commodity industry. AI can improve planning, quality, maintenance, compliance, and commercial workflows, but low labor intensity, environmental complexity, domestic resource pressure, and benchmark pricing constrain the benefit.
narrative.ai_impact: Practical applications include environmental and production records, laboratory exception review, maintenance knowledge, scheduling, inventory, procurement, and customer documentation. Acid production, reaction, granulation, material handling, inspections, and safety-critical decisions remain operator-controlled.
narrative.value_capture: Specialty specifications, qualification, logistics, and reliable capacity can protect some gain. Commodity phosphate prices, transparent tenders, input escalation, annual purchasing, and expanding global capacity cause substantial pass-through.
narrative.firm_availability: Only toll, private-label, and customer-specific manufacturers fit the outsourced-service lens. EPA's regulated-facility count and USGS mining concentration point to a small integrated universe, while broad owner aging is a weak proxy for transfers in corporate phosphate assets.
narrative.demand_durability: Phosphorus is essential to plant nutrition and physical fertilizer cannot be replaced by software. Yet 2025 US apparent phosphate-rock consumption fell, domestic reserves and ore grade face pressure, and imports have risen, supporting a mildly declining domestic base case.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Principal uncertainties are the scarce eligible subset, mine and plant integration, phosphate reserve quality, imports and global capacity, customer concentration, sulfur and ammonia costs, environmental liabilities, phosphogypsum management, and absent plant-task and contract data.
narrative.principal_driver: Essential recurring phosphorus demand preserves the need for accountable physical manufacturing.
narrative.principal_weakness: A concentrated commodity structure and unmeasured contract subset leave few credible LMM service targets.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 325312 Phosphatic Fertilizer Manufacturing
sources.supports: Defines the industry as manufacturing phosphatic fertilizer materials or manufacturing phosphatic materials and mixing them with other ingredients into fertilizers.

sources.title: Data tables for OEWS industry charts, May 2024
sources.supports: Lists chemical equipment operators, production supervisors, chemical plant operators, industrial machinery mechanics, and related plant roles among the largest occupations in chemical manufacturing groups including 3253.

sources.title: Producing the goods of the future: Job opportunities in manufacturing
sources.supports: Reports production occupations at about half of manufacturing employment in 2024 and connects automated-machinery adoption with continuing mechanic demand.

sources.title: Phosphate Fertilizer and Phosphoric Acid Manufacturing NESHAP
sources.supports: Reports 13 facilities subject to at least one relevant rule, describes covered process lines, and identifies regulated fluoride, particulate, and mercury emissions.

sources.title: Fertilizer Manufacturing Effluent Guidelines
sources.supports: Describes phosphate fertilizer production through sulfuric acid and wet-process phosphoric acid and identifies phosphoric acid, superphosphates, and ammonium phosphates as principal products.

sources.title: Business Owners' Ages: Over Half of U.S. Business Owners Were Age 55 and Over
sources.supports: Reports that 51% of responding owners of employer businesses were age 55 or older in the 2019 ABS, data year 2018.

sources.title: Mineral Commodity Summaries 2026: Phosphate Rock
sources.supports: Reports five companies at ten US mines, 20 million tons estimated 2025 production, 21 million tons apparent consumption versus 22.5 million in 2024, more than 95% used for phosphoric acid, rising imports, reserve and ore-grade pressure, and projected global P2O5 consumption growth.

sources.title: U.S. fertilizer consumption rebounds from 2021 drop
sources.supports: Reports total US fertilizer consumption history and that phosphate averaged 19% of fertilizer volume from 2006 through 2023.
